highlights.description: Organization rename from agentplexus to plexusone
Built-in slog provider for local trace logging during development and debugging
Structured evaluation integration for connecting llmops with sevaluation workflows

added.description: `llmops/slog` provider for local structured logging of trace events
`WithLogger` option in `ClientOptions` for custom slog.Logger configuration

fixed.description: gosec warnings for APIKey struct fields (G117) and HTTP client SSRF (G704)

documentation.description: README updated with slog provider in supported providers table
Updated all documentation to use new module path

highlights.description: Compatibility update for omnillm v0.11.0 with new multi-provider configuration API

changed.description: Updated `ClientConfig` usage to new `Providers` slice API for omnillm v0.11.0 compatibility

documentation.description: Converted CHANGELOG to schangelog JSON format with commit references

highlights.description: Comprehensive evaluation metrics for assessing LLM outputs with both code-based and LLM-based metrics
Streamlined provider architecture with Opik and Phoenix adapters moved to standalone SDKs

added.description: `AnnotationManager` interface for span/trace annotations with `CreateAnnotation` and `ListAnnotations` methods
OmniLLM hook auto-creates traces when none exists in context

changed.description: Provider adapters moved to standalone SDKs: Opik to `github.com/plexusone/go-opik/llmops`, Phoenix to `github.com/plexusone/go-phoenix/llmops`

fixed.description: Redundant newlines in example Println statements

highlights.description: Full-stack observability with new agentops and observops packages for monitoring agentic AI systems
Semantic conventions aligned with OpenTelemetry for standardized telemetry across LLM applications

added.description: `semconv` package with semantic conventions for agentic AI

highlights.description: Project renamed from observai to omniobserve for consistency with omnillm ecosystem naming

changed.description: Project renamed from `observai` to `omniobserve` for consistency with `omnillm` naming conventions

highlights.description: FluxLLM integration enables observability for flux-based LLM workflows
Initial release providing unified observability primitives for LLM applications with OpenTelemetry integration

added.description: Core observability framework for LLM applications
